message:
fix(session): prevent panic on fill_buf error during segment recovery

Replace bare `?` propagation on reader.fill_buf() with
.map(|b| b.is_empty()).unwrap_or(false) during error recovery in the
segment reader loop. When a frame parse fails and we check if we're
at EOF, the fill_buf() call itself could fail (e.g. on I/O error),
causing an unwanted early return that loses the partial-frame warning
context. Now we conservatively assume not-at-EOF on read errors,
allowing the warning log to fire with proper diagnostic context.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
